source_alignment.subject: Science
source_alignment.topic: Plant growth from seeds and bulbs and conditions for health
source_alignment.source_statement: Observe and describe how seeds and bulbs grow into mature plants; find out and describe how plants need water, light and a suitable temperature to grow and stay healthy.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack develops observation over time, life-stage sequencing and simple fair-comparison reasoning. It distinguishes germination from later healthy growth, treats outcomes as evidence rather than guaranteed rules, and uses simulated or adult-supported investigations without asking children to taste plants or handle unknown species.

objective.child_goal: I can describe how a seed or bulb grows and what helps a plant stay healthy.
objective.statement: Observe and sequence plant growth from seed or bulb and use evidence to describe the roles of water, light and suitable temperature.
misconceptions: Thinks a seed is not alive because it is small, dry and not moving.
Believes a seed or bulb already contains a tiny fully formed adult plant.
Thinks soil is food that a plant eats or that every plant must grow in soil.
Changes several conditions at once and then claims one condition caused the result.
Assumes more water or more heat always makes a plant grow better.
Thinks plants do not need light because a seed can begin germinating in darkness.
Judges plant health from height alone and ignores colour, leaf strength or wilting.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A living structure that can begin a new plant when conditions are suitable.
vocabulary.audio_script: A seed can stay quiet for a time and then begin to grow.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A bean seed opening as the first root emerges.

vocabulary.child_explanation: An underground plant structure that stores food and can grow a new shoot.
vocabulary.audio_script: A bulb stores food and can grow roots and a shoot.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: An onion-like bulb with roots below and a shoot above.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Begin to grow from a seed.
vocabulary.audio_script: Germinate means a seed starts growing.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A seed coat opening beside a first-root icon.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A plant that has grown through its earlier stages.
vocabulary.audio_script: A mature plant is a plant that has developed beyond its young stages.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A sequence ending with a larger leafy or flowering plant.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A temperature that is not too hot or too cold for that plant.
vocabulary.audio_script: Suitable temperature means the warmth is right for the plant.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A thermometer with cool, suitable and too-hot labelled zones.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Growing and working well, with signs such as firm stems and suitable leaf colour.
vocabulary.audio_script: We use several observations to decide whether a plant looks healthy.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Height, leaf colour, leaf number and stem-strength evidence cards.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Look closely at the seed and bulb. What can you observe without guessing?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Retrieve observation language and separate visible evidence from assumptions.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Say what you can see, feel or measure safely. Do not say what is inside unless evidence shows it.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Large seed and bulb images sit beside SEE, FEEL WITH PERMISSION and MEASURE icons.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Put the pictures in order from seed to mature plant.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Establish growth as a gradual sequence with roots and shoots developing over time.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The first root usually grows down. The shoot grows up. Leaves open as the young plant develops.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Four dated observation cards show seed, first root, shoot with leaves and mature plant.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Compare day 3 and day 7. What changed, and what stayed the same?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model specific comparative observation using dates, simple measures and plant features.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: On day seven the shoot is taller and has two leaves. It is still the same bean plant.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two photo cards align beside a ruler, leaf counter and sentence frame.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Keep the plants the same, change one condition, then predict and observe.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce simple fair comparison for water, light or temperature.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Change one thing so we can use the result as evidence about that condition.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two matched plants share identical icons except for one highlighted condition card.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: A seed sprouted in a dark cupboard. Does that prove the plant never needs light?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Diagnose confusion between early germination and conditions for continued healthy growth.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A seed can use stored food to begin growing. Watch what happens to the young plant over more time without light.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A short pale shoot in darkness is compared with later healthy and light-deprived observations.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Pip why giving a plant lots and lots of water may not keep it healthy.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Repair the linear more-is-better misconception and secure suitable-condition language.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Plants need enough water, but too much can stop roots getting the conditions they need.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Dry, suitable and waterlogged pots display evidence cards rather than happy/sad faces.

manipulatives.purpose: Sequence dated plant observations and record visible changes using pictures, measures, counts and spoken or written notes.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners order cards, compare two dates, add a ruler or leaf count, and complete an observation sentence. Photos may be simulated or supplied by an adult-led class investigation.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Audio description, image zoom, high-contrast ruler, tactile/offline matching cards, speech-to-text, adult scribing and symbol sentence frames support varied sensory, language and motor access.

manipulatives.purpose: Plan and inspect simple comparisons involving water, light or suitable temperature.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children match two plants, lock controlled conditions, change one condition, predict, then compare time-stamped outcomes. The tool distinguishes germination observations from later health observations.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: A linear question route replaces the spatial lab, all changes are textually announced, reduced-motion mode uses static photo panels, and no real investigation requires handling soil, unknown plants, hot equipment or tasting.

practice.interleaving_notes: Begin with clear seed/bulb growth sequences and specific observations. Then test one need at a time before interleaving germination, continued healthy growth, fair comparison and multi-feature health evidence.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Order and describe visible stages as seeds and bulbs develop into mature plants.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: 3-5 dated images + root/shoot/leaf observations + first/next/later language
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use biologically plausible sequences from varied familiar plants and avoid implying every species develops at the same rate.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Record and compare plant changes using dates, simple measures and more than one visible feature.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two observation cards + height/leaf/colour/stem evidence + oral or constructed comparison
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Images need scale cues and accessible descriptions; accept qualitative comparison when measurement access is limited.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Use matched comparisons to investigate water, light or suitable temperature as plant conditions.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two matched plants + one changed condition + prediction + time-stamped evidence + cautious conclusion
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep claims within the evidence, distinguish growth medium from plant food, and use safe simulated or adult-led contexts.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Distinguish conditions that start germination from evidence about continued healthy growth.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: early and later timeline + water/light/temperature evidence + best explanation choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use age-appropriate language and avoid absolute claims across all plant species.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retain plant-stage, need and suitable-condition reasoning through mixed spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue + dry/suitable/excess or cold/suitable/hot comparison + multi-feature evidence
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not use emotive dying animations; show evidence and use may/can language where outcomes vary.

hints: The first root comes out before the leafy shoot is tall.
The mature plant belongs last.
question_variants.explanation: The seed germinates, a first root emerges, the shoot and leaves develop, and the plant grows towards maturity.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "seed", "first root", "shoot with first leaves", "larger mature plant". The seed germinates, a first root emerges, the shoot and leaves develop, and the plant grows towards maturity.
feedback.repair: The first root comes out before the leafy shoot is tall. The mature plant belongs last. Use that clue to repair tiny adult inside, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this growth sequence, use the observable feature, ordered model, comparison or test result rather than a guess. The scientific evidence supports value: "seed", "first root", "shoot with first leaves", "larger mature plant": The seed germinates, a first root emerges, the shoot and leaves develop, and the plant grows towards maturity.

body.prompt: Growth trail 1: put the bean picture cards in first-to-later order.
body.season_note: Classroom timing is one example and is not a fixed timetable for every plant.
body.sentence_scaffold: First I notice __. Next __ appears. Later the plant has __.
body.tactile_alternative: Use five thick cards with a seed-or-bulb shape, root line, shoot line and leaf textures.
body.practical_safety: Use clean photographs or teacher-provided specimens; do not taste seeds, bulbs or plant parts.

hints: Find the seed or bulb before looking for a root.
Next find the first root, then follow the shoot and leaves.
question_variants.explanation: dry bean seed → first root emerges → shoot rises → leaves open → larger bean plant. The root emerges before the shoot and leaves develop. Different plants can grow at different rates.

feedback.correct: Trail complete. You used visible stages to place growth forwards.
feedback.repair: Keep the seed first. Look for the first root before the leafy stages.
feedback.evidence: dry bean seed → first root emerges → shoot rises → leaves open → larger bean plant. The root emerges before the shoot and leaves develop. Different plants can grow at different rates.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the seed is not living idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks a seed is not alive because it is small, dry and not moving.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner classifies a viable seed as never alive based only on a still observation.
repair_sequence: Compare a dry seed with dated germination observations.
Name the suitable conditions that allowed change to begin.
Explain that living things can have quiet stages.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner uses the germination sequence as evidence that a new seed is living.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Believes a seed or bulb already contains a tiny fully formed adult plant.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner describes growth only as a hidden adult getting bigger and ignores developing roots, shoots and leaves.
repair_sequence: Order close-up stage images.
Mark each newly visible structure.
Describe development as parts emerging and changing over time.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner describes at least two new structures in a fresh growth sequence.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks soil is food that a plant eats or that every plant must grow in soil.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner selects soil as the plant's food and cannot distinguish growth medium from water, light and stored seed food.
repair_sequence: Observe roots taking in water from a growth medium.
Compare a teacher-approved soil and non-soil growing example.
Use the sentence frame: the medium supports roots; the plant needs water, light and suitable temperature.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner explains the role of a growth medium without calling it food.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Changes several conditions at once and then claims one condition caused the result.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner changes water, light, plant type or temperature together and still names one cause with certainty.
repair_sequence: Choose the one condition to investigate.
Match the plants and lock the other condition cards.
Compare the outcomes and make a conclusion limited to that test.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner plans a new comparison with one changed condition and names what must stay alike.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Assumes more water or more heat always makes a plant grow better.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner always selects the maximum condition despite evidence of wilting, yellowing or weak growth.
repair_sequence: Compare too little, suitable and too much conditions.
Inspect more than one health feature.
Replace 'more' with 'a suitable amount or temperature' in the conclusion.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner chooses and justifies a suitable middle condition in a new comparison.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks plants do not need light because a seed can begin germinating in darkness.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner generalises one early sprout observation to the plant's whole life.
repair_sequence: Separate the early germination and later growth cards.
Track the dark-grown shoot over more time.
Compare leaf colour and strength with a light-grown plant.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner distinguishes beginning germination from continued healthy growth.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Judges plant health from height alone and ignores colour, leaf strength or wilting.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner labels a tall pale weak plant healthier than a shorter green firm plant solely because it is taller.
repair_sequence: List height, colour, leaves and stem condition separately.
Compare all evidence cards.
Make a cautious health judgement using two or more observations.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner cites multiple visible features when comparing two new plants.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm natural images, no distressing decay close-ups, no sudden wilting animation and optional ambient sound.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use static dated growth cards and before-and-after condition panels instead of time-lapse, moving roots or wilting effects.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Narrate every stage, date, measurement and condition; provide replay and audio descriptions that do not disclose the answer.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Pair short sentences with icons, use first-next-later frames, adjustable spacing and a persistent seed-root-shoot-leaf glossary.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Show one observation or one changed condition at a time using OBSERVE-COMPARE-EXPLAIN.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow image zoom, unlimited viewing time, reduced choices, physical card alternatives and adult-supported observation recording.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Treat predictions as ideas to test, preserve correct observations during repair and avoid scoring plant-care outcomes as personal success or failure.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach seed, bulb, root, shoot, leaf, light, water, warm, healthy and suitable with real objects or pictures; allow home-language observation before English sentence frames.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires sequencing seed or bulb growth and describing water, light and suitable temperature with observation support; secure requires retained fair-comparison, germination-versus-health and suitable-condition reasoning using more than one evidence feature.
evidence.parent_summary: Can describe how seeds and bulbs grow and use observations to explain what helps plants stay healthy.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Orders growth stages, records dated changes, identifies a fair one-condition comparison and explains plant health using water, light, suitable temperature and multiple observations.
next_actions: Continue a safe adult-supported plant observation journal over several weeks.
Revisit conclusions when only one plant or one short observation period was used.
Connect Year 2 growth evidence to Year 3 plant-part functions without teaching later mechanisms too early.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 2 plants pack with a deterministic 220-item pilot bank. Five curated variants are preserved alongside candidates covering seed and bulb growth, germination, dated observation, fair comparisons, suitable conditions, seasonal context and misconception repair. Every generated item includes SEND scaffolds, visual or tactile alternatives, supported response routes, evidence-rich feedback, safety-aware practical guidance and untimed low-pressure progress. Independent science, teacher, accessibility, safeguarding and renderer review is still required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
